result.raw_english: Exercise is a key part of maintaining good health, especially with your disciplined and health-focused approach. Aim for at least 150 minutes of moderate activity each week, like brisk walking or cycling, combined with strength training twice a week to build muscle and support metabolism. To fit exercise into a busy career-oriented lifestyle, try breaking sessions into manageable 10-15 minute blocks throughout the day. Consistency and variety help keep motivation high and prevent burnout. If you’re starting a new routine or have any health concerns, consulting a healthcare professional can ensure your plan is safe and effective.
result.remodeled_english:
Exercise plays a vital role in staying healthy, especially for someone disciplined and health-focused like you. Aim for at least 150 minutes of moderate activity each week—such as brisk walking or cycling—and add strength training twice a week to build muscle and boost metabolism. To fit exercise into a busy career-driven schedule, try breaking your workouts into 10-15 minute sessions throughout the day. Staying consistent and mixing up activities will help keep you motivated and prevent burnout.
